subject: Geology
title: Mountain Landscapes
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Mountain Landscapes.

hook.text: Mountains rise by tectonic force but are shaped by weathering and erosion. Their peaks are a battle between up and down.

buildup.text: Mountain landscapes reflect the balance between uplift and erosion. Sharp peaks mean uplift wins; rounded hills don't.

discovery.text: Glaciers carve jagged peaks and U-shaped valleys. Rivers cut V-shaped valleys into rising mountain blocks.

twist.text: The Appalachians were once as tall as the Himalayas. Hundreds of millions of years of erosion wore them down.

climax.text: Mountains are temporary. Given enough time, even the tallest range will be worn flat by weathering and erosion.

punchline.text: Every mountain is slowly being erased.

quiz.question: What shaped the Appalachian Mountains into their current rounded form?
options: Hundreds of millions of years of erosion
